task: Open the contacts app. Clear any pop-ups that may appear by granting all permissions that are required.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The Contacts app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Contacts app → | Step 2: Handle any permission pop-ups by granting required permissions | Step 3: Verify the Contacts app is open and ready

[Decision]
Based on the objective to open the Contacts app, and since it is not visible on the current home screen page, I will use a system-level open command to launch it directly, bypassing the need to navigate through app drawers or search manually.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The screen displays the main interface of the Contacts app. The contact list is visible, showing one contact named "Emilia Gonzalez". There are no permission pop-ups or dialogs present on the screen; the interface appears fully loaded and ready.

[Reflection]
The previous plan anticipated handling permission pop-ups in Step 2. However, the app opened directly to the main contacts list without any permission requests appearing.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Contacts app ✓ | Step 2: Verify the app is open and ready (no pop-ups) ✓ | Step 3: Complete task →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Contacts app is successfully open and displaying the main interface. Since the requirement was to open the app and clear any pop-ups by granting permissions, and there are no pop-ups to handle, the task objectives have been fully met. I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Contacts app opened successfully; no permission pop-ups appeared upon launch. Task objectives fulfilled.
